basics.name: Sakin Kirti
basics.label: Computational Biologist
basics.summary: Building and applying machine learning to understand and model biology.

work.name: Cardiovascular Disease Initiative, The Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard
work.summary: Uncovering novel gene targets for dilated cardiomyopathy treatment. Building next-generation contractility platform.
highlights: Applying DINO-ViT to uncover novel gene targets for treatment of DCM
Developing methods to mitigate batch effects in imaging features
Building the analysis-half of the next generation high-throughput contractility platform
Mentored undergraduate students through the Broad's BSRP summer program

work.name: Center for Computational Imaging and Personalized Diagnostics, Case Western Reserve University
work.summary: Identifying racial biases in prostate cancer diagnosis.
highlights: Trained SqueezeNet models on race-stratified prostate cancer MRI datasets
Used activation maps to observe image areas that networks were using for diagnosis
Identified racial biases by comparing model metrics and activation maps

work.name: Atit Lab, Department of Biology, Case Western Reserve University
work.summary: Wet lab + computational approaches to fibrosis discovery
highlights: Beckman Scholar award ($20,000/15 mo) for biology research - awarded 2020
Carried out project identifying the key role of DPP4 in skin fibrosis progression
Predicted skin fibrosis with convolutional neural network and using radiomics approaches
Developed FIJI plug-ins to analyze lab's imaging experiments in an automated, unbiased fashion

work.name: Surgo Ventures
highlights: Developed probabilistic graphical modeling (PGM) package to computationally experiment with interventions
Used PGM package to model maternal mortality; verified transportation as key roadblock for maternal doctor visits
Built multilevel regression model to analyze maternal transportation experiment with Uber Health

work.name: Heller Lab, Department of Otolaryngology, Stanford University School of Medicine
work.summary: In-situ hybridizations/differential gene expression for hearing regeneration
highlights: Performed in-sity hybridizations to identify localization of hearing cell regeneration in chicken cochlea
Performed sequencing and plotting of scRNA-seq data to identify key genes in hearing cell regeneration

work.name: Li Lab, Department of Biology, University of Massachusetts-Amherst
work.summary: Patch clamping to identify calcium reuptake in bullfrog papilla
highlights: Performed patch clamping to measure calcium ion release and reuptake

volunteer.summary: Lead organizer for the New York City branch of the People's Climate March, the largest climate march in history.
highlights: Awarded 'Climate Hero' award by Greenpeace for my efforts organizing the march.
Men of the year 2014 by Time magazine

awards.summary: For top 100 students after their sophomore year at CWRU

awards.summary: $21,000 funded 'research experience for exceptional undergraduate students in chemistry, biological sciences, or interdisciplinary combinations thereof'

awards.title: Best Undergraduate Poster Presentation
awards.summary: Best undergraduate poster presentation at conference

certificates.name: Statistical Analysis with R for Public Health

publications.name: Zur Elektrody/namik bewegter Körper
publications.summary: It concerned an interpretation of the Michelson–Morley experiment and the properties of light and time. Special relativity incorporates the principle that the speed of light is the same for all inertial observers regardless of the state of motion of the source.

publications.name: Über einen die Erzeugung und Verwandlung des Lichtes betreffenden heuristischen Gesichtspunkt
publications.summary: In the second paper, he applied the quantum theory to light to explain the photoelectric effect. In particular, he used the idea of light quanta (photons) to explain experimental results, but stressed the importance of the experimental results. The importance of his work on the photoelectric effect earned him the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1921.

publications.name: Die Grundlage der allgemeinen Relativitätstheorie
publications.summary: The publication of the theory of general relativity made him internationally famous. He was professor of physics at the universities of Zurich (1909–1911) and Prague (1911–1912), before he returned to ETH Zurich (1912–1914).

projects.name: Quantum Computing
projects.summary: Quantum computing is the use of quantum-mechanical phenomena such as superposition and entanglement to perform computation. Computers that perform quantum computations are known as quantum computers.
highlights: Quantum Teleportation
Quantum Cryptography
